title: McCarthy: Trump's Avatar
summary: "The remark (and others) demonstrated not only that the Republican Party had allowed to be nominated a man lacking the usual dignity expected in an American presidential candidate; but also, that American public discourse had developed a coarseness over the recent decades that is a disturbing development to those who have a sense of what American history is all about."
body: Note An earlier version of this commentary appeared on The World Post . The latest from Belfer See more The Merriam-Webster dictionary lists one of the definitions of avatar as the following: "Someone who represents a type of person, an idea or a quality." In terms of reckless mendaciousness, though not of vulgarity, Donald J. Trump, the current Republican Candidate for President, is certainly reminiscent of the late Senator Joseph McCarthy. On June 9, 1954, at a Congressional hearing, McCarthy sought to smear as a pro-Communist Boston lawyer Fred Fisher. Joseph Welch, a senior member of the same Boston law firm, Hale and Dorr, and a participant at the hearing, admonished the Wisconsin Senator: "Have You No Sense of Decency, Sir?" These ringing words at the Army-McCarthy hearings were the ones that sank the Wisconsin Senator for good and sent him into political perdition accompanied by creeping alcoholism. The modern equivalent, in that it definitively ended Donald Trump's campaign for the presidency, was his bus tour comment that, because he is a star, he can treat any woman he meets as he wants, including grabbing them "by the pussy." This heretofore non-word in serious public discourse was enough to send the Trump candidacy into oblivion. Trump had said things of dubious taste during the campaign, but this was way over the top. The remark (and others) demonstrated not only that the Republican Party had allowed to be nominated a man lacking the usual dignity expected in an American presidential candidate; but also, that American public discourse had developed a coarseness over the recent decades that is a disturbing development to those who have a sense of what American history is all about. Recommended citation Cogan, Dr. Charles G. "McCarthy: Trump's Avatar." Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School, October 26, 2016. result.main_content_en: The article draws a direct parallel between former President Donald Trump and the late Senator Joseph McCarthy, using the concept of an 'avatar' to signify Trump as a modern embodiment of McCarthy's reckless mendacity and disregard for decency. It highlights the pivotal moments that ended each man's political momentum: for McCarthy, it was the famous 'Have you no sense of decency, sir?' rebuke during the Army-McCarthy hearings; for Trump, it was the release of the 'Access Hollywood' tape containing vulgar comments about women. The commentary argues that these incidents revealed not only the candidates' profound lack of dignity but also a troubling coarsening of American public discourse over recent decades, representing a significant departure from historical norms.
result.main_content_zh: 本文直接将美国前总统唐纳德·特朗普与已故参议员约瑟夫·麦卡锡相提并论，用“化身”这一概念意指特朗普是现代版麦卡锡，体现了其鲁莽的虚伪和对体面的漠视。文章强调了终结两人政治势头的关键时刻：对麦卡锡而言，是陆军-麦卡锡听证会上著名的“先生，你难道没有一点廉耻吗？”的斥责；对特朗普而言，则是包含对女性粗俗言论的《走进好莱坞》录音带的曝光。评论认为，这些事件不仅揭示了候选人严重缺乏尊严，也表明了美国公共话语在近几十年来令人不安的粗鄙化趋势，这代表了与历史规范的显著背离。
